comment_text.sn14.3:1.2: This discourse asserts that, in this case, conditionality is one way. This heads off the assumption that mutual condition applies to all the factors (SN 12.65:2.16).

root_text.sn14.3:1.2: “Dhātunānattaṁ, bhikkhave, paṭicca uppajjati phassanānattaṁ, no phassanānattaṁ paṭicca uppajjati dhātunānattaṁ.
root_text.sn14.3:2.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, dhātunānattaṁ paṭicca uppajjati phassanānattaṁ, no phassanānattaṁ paṭicca uppajjati dhātunānattaṁ?
root_text.sn14.3:2.2: Cakkhudhātuṁ, bhikkhave, paṭicca uppajjati cakkhusamphasso, no cakkhusamphassaṁ paṭicca uppajjati cakkhudhātu …pe…
root_text.sn14.3:2.3: manodhātuṁ paṭicca uppajjati manosamphasso, no manosamphassaṁ paṭicca uppajjati manodhātu.
root_text.sn14.3:2.4: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, dhātunānattaṁ paṭicca uppajjati phassanānattaṁ, no phassanānattaṁ paṭicca uppajjati dhātunānattan”ti.

translation_text.sn14.3:1.2: “Mendicants, diversity of elements gives rise to diversity of contacts. Diversity of contacts doesn’t give rise to diversity of elements.
translation_text.sn14.3:1.3: And what is the diversity of elements?
translation_text.sn14.3:1.4: The eye element, ear element, nose element, tongue element, body element, and mind element.
translation_text.sn14.3:1.6: This is called the diversity of elements.
translation_text.sn14.3:2.1: And how does diversity of elements give rise to diversity of contacts, while diversity of contacts doesn’t give rise to diversity of elements?
translation_text.sn14.3:2.2: The eye element gives rise to eye contact. Eye contact doesn’t give rise to the eye element. …
translation_text.sn14.3:2.3: The mind element gives rise to mind contact. Mind contact doesn’t give rise to the mind element.
translation_text.sn14.3:2.4: That’s how diversity of elements gives rise to diversity of contacts, while diversity of contacts doesn’t give rise to diversity of elements.”
